## Chronology Spine

## Core Truth Set
- (5/5) Group formation and inter-group distance operate as governance signals; spacing itself carries political meaning. | Quote: "The spacing between groups mattered most ... The distance itself held meaning."
- (5/5) Assembly uses multiple low controlled fires instead of a single center, distributing attention and reducing singular authority cues. | Quote: "Not one central flame. Many. Each low. Each controlled."
- (5/5) The gathering site is framed as shared, held ground rather than proprietary territory, enabling multi-group coexistence. | Quote: "The land did not belong to anyone. That was why it held them all."
- (4/5) Environmental softening (smoke diffusion, layered sound) is treated as procedural delay, making immediate clarity intentionally unavailable. | Quote: "Clarity was not meant to come quickly here."
- (4/5) Multiple unmarked but visible paths indicate adaptive network memory across regular, occasional, and seasonal routes. | Quote: "Paths entered from every direction ... Not marked ... But visible."

## Setting Anchors
- Held Multi-Group Clearing [FIELD]: Slightly raised convergence ground with distributed entry paths and layered fire/smoke conditions for negotiated assembly.

## Continuity Constraints
- Spacing as Diplomatic Language: Spatial diplomacy constraint.
- Distributed Attention Management: Attention-governance constraint.
- Entry Pause Awareness Protocol: Entry-sequencing constraint.

## Open Research Gaps
- What practical rules govern placement and count of low fires in multi-group gatherings? (priority 2): Needed to keep assembly staging materially coherent.
- How do seasonal path changes alter who can arrive, when, and with what relational leverage? (priority 2): Needed to connect route ecology with gathering power dynamics.
- What spacing thresholds map to alliance, neutrality, and tension states in these assemblies? (priority 2): Needed for consistent spatial coding of political relations across scenes.

## Next Chapter Assembly Rule
- Do not promote a chapter beat to the core story unless it references at least one core claim, one chronology anchor (or explicit open gap), and one continuity constraint.
